headline: Christian Jambet, Islamic scholar: 'The true leaders of Iran are now soldiers who want to spread the Islamic Revolution beyond Iran's border'
description: The philosopher and member of the Académie Française, an expert on Shiite spiritualities, shares his thoughts on the ongoing war and the 'degeneration' of the Iranian regime.

headline: Escaping Putin's Russia: A father and daughter persecuted over a drawing, now in exile in France
Anne Hidalgo's unwavering tenacity after 12 years as Paris mayor
Israeli police prevent Jerusalem's Latin Patriarch from entering Holy Sepulchre on Palm Sunday
description: French President Emmanuel Macron condemned this action, saying that worship 'for all religions' must be guaranteed in Jerusalem. Israeli police said all holy sites in Jerusalem have been closed since the start of the Middle East war.

headline: Two more suspects arrested in foiled Paris bomb attack outside Bank of America
description: Police had detained a first suspect, possibly a minor, in the early hours of Saturday just after he placed a device outside the bank building near the Champs-Elysées. He was accompanied by a second person who fled when police arrived.

headline: The American dream crumbles for French students
description: Since Donald Trump's return to the White House, several institutions have reported a decline in applications to US campuses. Amid political tensions, stricter visa scrutiny and a shift favoring colleges in Asia, a subtle downturn is taking shape.

headline: 'The use of lethal autonomous weapons controlled by AI goes against the principles of a just war'
description: In both Iran and Gaza, the rise in targeted strikes carried out by artificial intelligence systems already has serious consequences for the fundamental principle of civilian protection, philosopher Marie-des-Neiges Ruffo de Calabre tells Le Monde in an interview.

headline: Emmanuel Grégoire inaugurated mayor of Paris
'No Kings' protests against Trump focus on war in Iran: 'The US is on the wrong side of history'
In Nepal, popular former rapper Balendra Shah, 35, becomes prime minister
France condemns Houthis for entering Middle East war
Iranians live under constant anxiety: 'When I close my eyes, I wonder if I will see the next day'
In Monaco, Pope Leo XIV delivers a message on wealth redistribution: 'Every good entrusted to us has a universal destination'
Substack, a digital refuge for writers, accelerates its growth in France
War returns to southern Lebanon: 'Southern people have endured so many sacrifices'
Iran's Revolutionary Guard threatens US universities in the Middle East
Matisse, the carver of light, at the Grand Palais in Paris
Revealed: Satellite image shows mysterious cargo at Iranian nuclear site in 2025
Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
In Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, 'no one ventures outside anymore apart from rescue teams'
description: Like 42 other rescue workers since the war began on March 2, 16-year-old Joude Souleiman was killed by an Israeli drone strike in Nabatieh, the second-largest Shiite city in southern Lebanon, where only a few hundred people out of 75,000 residents remain.

headline: The serial-poisoning mother and daughter inspired by 'Breaking Bad'
description: Castor beans, wolfsbane, antifreeze, rat poison: Carole D. and Amélie D. considered every possible method to poison the former's partner, Enrique B. The two women are set to stand trial before a criminal court in western France starting March 25.

headline: From afar, American students in France feel the shockwaves of Trump
description: As academic freedom declines in the US, some young people are choosing France to pursue master's and doctoral degrees. Yet, between delayed scholarships, intrusive questions and stereotypes, their experience remains shaken by the latest developments in their country.

headline: Ukrainians' suffering must not be forgotten amid the war against Iran
Pierre Buhler, former diplomat: We are witnessing 'a worrying crack in the European edifice'
The French people's vote is still up for grabs
